weather_report:
Late morning in Cottesloe arrives with a rinsed, salt-bright sky and light showers brushing through, the kind that whisper on the footpath and leave the air smelling clean. Expect the rain to taper after lunch, giving way to scattered clouds and shy breaks of sun that silver the water without ever quite blazing. It won’t be a notably sunny day, more a soft one, with gentle light and workable visibility; the horizon may feel a touch gauzy until the afternoon opens a few windows of clarity. The winter sun keeps the UV on the mild side, but when the clouds thin it still has a quiet bite, enough to warrant sunglasses and a dab of sunscreen if you linger.

Dress in layers and a light rain jacket, comfortable shoes you don’t mind getting damp, and tuck a compact umbrella in your bag. It’s a good day for a slow coffee under an awning, a quick loop along Marine Parade between showers, or a meander through the grocer for something warm to simmer later. If you’re working, set up by a window and let the softened daylight do its steady work; if you’re free, a library visit or a small gallery wander suits the mood. As the clouds loosen in the afternoon, take ten unrushed minutes on a bench above the beach and watch the whitecaps keep their patient rhythm.
